# onelogin_role (Data Source)

Use this data source to look up a OneLogin Role by ID or by name. Exactly one of `id` or `name` must be specified.

## Example Usage

### Lookup by ID

```terraform
data "onelogin_role" "example" {
  id = 12345
}

output "role_name" {
  value = data.onelogin_role.example.name
}
```

### Lookup by name

```terraform
data "onelogin_role" "example" {
  name = "Engineering"
}

output "role_id" {
  value = data.onelogin_role.example.id
}
```

### Reference a role ID in a user mapping

```terraform
data "onelogin_role" "engineering" {
  name = "Engineering"
}

resource "onelogin_user_mapping" "engineering" {
  name    = "Auto-assign Engineering"
  match   = "any"
  enabled = true

  conditions {
    source   = "member_of"
    operator = "~"
    value    = "eng_all_staff"
  }

  actions {
    action = "set_role"
    value  = [tostring(data.onelogin_role.engineering.id)]
  }
}
```

> **Note on name-based lookup:** Name-based lookups pass a `?name=` query filter
> to the OneLogin API, which returns only matching roles without paginating through
> the full role list. This is efficient even for accounts with hundreds of roles.

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Optional

- `id` (Number) The ID of the role. Exactly one of `id` or `name` must be set.
- `name` (String) The name of the role. Exactly one of `id` or `name` must be set.

### Read-Only

- `admins` (Set of Number) A set of user IDs who administer this role.
- `apps` (Set of Number) A set of app IDs accessible by this role.
- `users` (Set of Number) A set of user IDs assigned to this role.

When looking up by `name`, the resolved integer `id` is always populated in state
and can be used by downstream resources.
